hypothesis: If crystal structures are jointly represented as graphs, RoboCrystallographer text, and simulated XRD spectra and trained with cross-modal predictive, VIC, and contrastive losses so that any two modalities predict the third in latent space, then downstream prediction MAE for band gap, shear modulus, bulk modulus, dielectric constant, and perovskite formation energy on Matbench and AFLOW will be lower than CGCNN, CoGN, and ALIGNN baselines because cross-modal alignment fuses complementary structure information into a shared representation.
